solution.p: There are at least two methods to reduce the fraction \displaystyle{ \frac{210}{63} }.
Method 1:We find a number that divides into both the numerator 210, and the denominator 63.
Check the first few prime numbers one by one: 2, 3, 5, 7, \ldots
In this case, 3 divides into both the numerator and denominator of \displaystyle{ \frac{210}{63} }. We divide 3 into both numbers, and we have:

Next, check again whether any prime number divides into both the numerator and denominator. We need to keep trying until no prime numbers divide into both numbers. In this problem, 7 divides into both the numerator and denominator for \displaystyle{ \frac{70}{21} }. We divide 7 into both numbers, and we have:
A second method to reduce fraction is to prime factor both the numerator and the denominator, and then cancel out factors in pairs: one from the numerator and one from the denominator.
